title: Make Rational fields private to enforce normalization invariant
analysis:
The Rational struct in crates/reify-types/src/dimension.rs has pub fields `num` and `den`, allowing external code to construct non-canonical values like `Rational { num: 2, den: 4 }` which bypasses GCD normalization in `Rational::new()`. This breaks equality and hashing silently.

All direct field accesses (.num, .den) and all struct literal constructions (`Rational { num: ..., den: ... }`) are confined to dimension.rs itself — no external crate accesses fields directly. The only external usage (crates/reify-stdlib/src/lib.rs:597) uses `Rational::new(1, 2)` which is already safe.

The fix is contained entirely within dimension.rs:
1. Remove `pub` from num/den fields
2. Add getter methods num() and den()
3. Make gcd() and new() const fn (Rust edition 2024 supports this) so const contexts can use Rational::new() instead of struct literals
4. Replace all struct literal constructions with Rational::new() calls
5. Replace all direct field accesses in methods with getter calls
6. Update test assertions from Rational { num: X, den: Y } to Rational::new(X, Y)

Since all code is within the same module (dimension.rs and its child `tests` mod), Rust's privacy rules mean the code would still compile with private fields. But the task asks us to also update internal usages to go through getters/new() for consistency and to model the correct pattern.

prerequisites.description: Verify all existing dimension tests pass as a baseline before refactoring: `cargo test -p reify-types -- dimension`

steps.description: Add failing tests for getter methods: `rational_getters` tests that `Rational::new(3, 4).num() == 3` and `.den() == 4`, and `rational_normalization_via_getters` tests that `Rational::new(2, 4).num() == 1` and `.den() == 2`. These will fail to compile because num() and den() methods don't exist yet.
Add pub getter methods `pub fn num(&self) -> i16` and `pub fn den(&self) -> i16` to Rational impl. Tests from step-1 should now pass.

Add test `rational_const_new_normalization` that verifies const construction: `const R: Rational = Rational::new(6, 4); assert_eq!(R.num(), 3); assert_eq!(R.den(), 2);`. This will fail because Rational::new() is not currently const fn.
Make `gcd` a const fn and `Rational::new` a const fn. The test from step-3 should now pass. This enables all const contexts to use Rational::new() instead of struct literals.

Make num and den fields private (remove `pub` keyword). Update all internal code:
- ZERO/ONE constants: use Rational::new(0,1) and Rational::new(1,1)
- Neg impl: Rational::new(-self.num(), self.den())
- basis_n: Rational::new(n, 1)
- pow: Rational::new(n, 1) and use getters for field access
- FORCE const: Rational::new(-2, 1)
- is_zero/is_integer/as_i8: use self.num()/self.den()
- Add/Sub impls: use .num()/.den() getters
- Display impl: use self.num()/self.den()
- DimensionVector::root: use .num()/.den() getters
- DimensionVector::pow: use .num()/.den() getters
- content_hash: use .num()/.den() getters
- Update all test assertions from `Rational { num: X, den: Y }` to `Rational::new(X, Y)`

All existing and new tests should pass.

design_decisions.decision: Make gcd() and Rational::new() const fn
design_decisions.rationale: Several const contexts (ZERO, ONE, basis_n, FORCE) currently use struct literal construction. Making new() const allows these to use Rational::new() as the single construction path, fulfilling the task requirement that 'The only construction path should be Rational::new(num, den) and derived constants.' Rust 2024 edition supports const fn with loops and assert!.

design_decisions.decision: Replace all internal field accesses with getter methods
design_decisions.rationale: While same-module code can access private fields, using getters consistently models the correct pattern and makes it clear that field access should go through the API. This follows the task requirement to 'Update all direct field accesses across the codebase to use getters.'

design_decisions.decision: Use Rational::new() in Neg impl instead of raw struct literal
design_decisions.rationale: While negating a normalized rational's numerator preserves normalization (so struct literal is technically safe), using Rational::new() is consistent with the single-construction-path principle. The cost of re-normalizing is negligible for i16 GCD.

design_decisions.decision: Update test assertions to use Rational::new() instead of struct literals
design_decisions.rationale: Tests in the child `mod tests` can still access private fields, but using Rational::new() in assertions is more idiomatic and demonstrates the intended API usage. All test values used (128/1, 1/64, 1/192, 100/1, 200/1, -128/1) are already in canonical form, so Rational::new() returns identical values.

reuse.how: This is already the canonical constructor. We make it const and route all construction through it.
Make it const fn — the while-loop implementation is already const-compatible in Rust 2024.
All existing tests serve as regression tests. We update struct literal assertions to use Rational::new() but the logic and coverage remain identical.
